channel.title: Protein Design Podcast
channel.description: Daily automated digest of protein design, antibody engineering & enzyme design research
channel.itunes:author: Eva Dai
channel.itunes:summary: Daily automated digest of protein design, antibody engineering & enzyme design research

item.title: Daily Podcast 2026-03-04
item.description: In a recent blog post on In the Pipeline, Derek Lowe explores how drug molecules can be designed to hide in unexpected places within biological systems. | A new study in PNAS introduces a unified graph neural network approach for predicting small molecule binding sites on proteins, addressing a critical gap in structure-based drug discovery. | Bi-TEAM is a unified framework that effectively combines the global biological context captured by protein language models with the fine-grained chemical precision of chemical language models, enabling robust representation learning for chemically modified biomolecules like those with non-canonical amino acids and post-translational modifications.
item.itunes:author: Eva Dai
item.itunes:summary: In a recent blog post on In the Pipeline, Derek Lowe explores how drug molecules can be designed to hide in unexpected places within biological systems. | A new study in PNAS introduces a unified graph neural network approach for predicting small molecule binding sites on proteins, addressing a critical gap in structure-based drug discovery. | Bi-TEAM is a unified framework that effectively combines the global biological context captured by protein language models with the fine-grained chemical precision of chemical language models, enabling robust representation learning for chemically modified biomolecules like those with non-canonical amino acids and post-translational modifications.
